items.title: Montreal film production lab to shut down, sparking calls from filmmakers for it to be saved
items.summary_auto: A Quebec facility that houses one of North America's only laboratories capable of developing film for professional productions is being shuttered, with the owner citing declining investment in local audiovisual productions as a factor.

items.title: Edmonton, central Alberta expected to get drenched with up to 70 mm of rain
items.summary_auto: After weeks of water-logged weather, communities in the Edmonton region and across central Alberta are again bracing for another severe rainstorm.

items.title: Calling all Janices! Drake wants to apologize by throwing you a party
items.summary_auto: If your name is Janice and you feel personally victimized by Drake’s song Janice STFU, you're in luck — provided you can show government-issued ID. Rumour has it, the Toronto-born rapper is ready to make amends with all Janices by throwing them an exclusive Janices-only apology party.

items.title: Registered sex offender pleads guilty to sexual assault of 3-year-old in Welland, Ont., other charges
items.summary_auto: Daniel Senecal was arrested on Aug. 31, 2025, after police were called to a home in Welland, Ont., for a "medical assistance call involving a child." The 26-year-old pleaded guilty this week in St. Catharines court to multiple charges.

items.title: Sherritt shutting down Alberta refinery as feed from Moa mine in Cuba runs out
items.summary_auto: Sherritt International Corp. is shutting down operations at its refinery in Fort Saskatchewan, Alta., after running out of the feed inventory it receives from its Moa mine in Cuba.

items.title: Sherritt shutting down Fort Saskatchewan, Alta., refinery as feed from Moa mine in Cuba runs out
items.summary_auto: Sherritt International Corp. is shutting down operations at its refinery in Fort Saskatchewan, Alta., after running out of the feed inventory it receives from its Moa mine in Cuba.

items.title: 10-day crackdown targets open drug use but not about 'criminalizing addiction': Winnipeg police
items.summary_auto: Winnipeg police say they have had more than 100 "interactions" and made two dozen arrests since the service ramped up patrolling in areas of the city affected by open drug use and trafficking earlier this week.

items.title: Israel, Lebanon sign framework agreement with U.S. in 'first step' toward peace, Rubio says
items.summary_auto: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio along with Israel and Lebanon's ambassadors announced a framework agreement Friday that was described as a first step toward peace following months of conflict between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah.

items.title: Love Island USA axes another contestant for using racial slur in resurfaced video post
items.summary_auto: Alannah Keyser, 21, has now become the second contestant to be removed from the current season of  Love Island USA  for using a racial slur in a resurfaced video online. The removal comes less than a week after the college student, who hails from Miami, entered as a bombshell during the Casa Amor episodes in the Peacock reality dating series.

items.title: Canada's most wanted fugitive, a Quebecer, arrested in Spain
items.summary_auto: All Boivin, considered the most wanted fugitive in Canada, has been arrested in Spain, according to Radio-Canada sources.

items.title: Small plane makes emergency landing on highway in western Manitoba
items.summary_auto: A pilot landed on a rural Manitoba highway Thursday night after issuing an alert to emergency officials.

items.title: John Bolton, former Trump adviser, pleads guilty to 1 count of illegally retaining classified info
items.summary_auto: John Bolton, national security adviser during Donald Trump's first presidential administration, pleaded guilty on Friday to illegally retaining classified information, sealing a deal with federal prosecutors.

items.title: Driver in Christmas vehicle attack in Germany that killed 6 people sentenced to life in prison
items.summary_auto: A Saudi doctor was sentenced to life in prison in Germany on ‌Friday for killing six people and injuring hundreds, after he rammed a rented BMW into crowds at a historic market in the eastern city of Magdeburg days before Christmas in 2024.

items.title: Loss of N.L. teen, father killed in moose crash 'deeply felt'
items.summary_auto: A community on the southern shore of the Avalon Peninsula is putting out hockey jerseys and gear in remembrance of a young woman and her father who were killed earlier this week.

items.title: RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements
items.summary_auto: Between 2001 and 2024, the FCAC says RBC failed to transfer credits from deactivated credit card accounts to customers' new ones. This resulted in incorrect monthly credit card statements.

items.title: Remember the P.E.I. restaurant with $0 rent and ocean views? It finally found an operator
items.summary_auto: A restaurant at Prince Edward Island’s northernmost point has found a new operator for this summer, after a public search that included a $0-rent offer.

items.title: Christine Sinclair, Michael J. Fox promoted to Order of Canada's highest level
items.summary_auto: Recently appointed Gov. Gen. Louise Arbour on Friday added 56 new Canadians to what is considered the country's highest civilian honour.

items.title: Serena Williams draws 20-year-old Maya Joint in first Wimbledon singles match in 4 years
items.summary_auto: Serena Williams will face an opponent less than half her age when she plays 20-year-old Maya Joint in the first round at Wimbledon for her first singles match in nearly four years.

items.title: Tears and champagne as Saskatoon runner finishes 1,500-km north-to-south run in memory of her mother
items.summary_auto: A Saskatoon teacher who just turned 38 — the same age her mother died from leukemia — marked the milestone by running 1,500 kilometres in a month, crossing Saskatchewan north to south. She reached the finish line at the Canada-U.S. border Thursday.

items.title: Alberta promising better care for cancer patients amid ongoing concerns about waits
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government says it is working to improve access to cancer care with a new 10-year strategic plan. It comes amid new cancer cases rising, health systems coming under increasing strain, and ongoing concerns over long waits for key cancer surgeries.

items.title: Russia reports large-scale Ukrainian drone attack across 12 regions
items.summary_auto: Russian air defences intercepted 660 Ukrainian drones in a major nighttime attack on 12 Russian regions as well as the Russia-held Crimean peninsula, the Black Sea and the Azov Sea, Russia's Defence Ministry said Friday.

items.title: We know violent and gory videos exist online. Teens are seeing more of them than you'd think
items.summary_auto: School fights. The Charlie Kirk killing. Violent encounters with police. A new survey of more than 1,000 teens across Canada offers a glimpse into the violent content they say they're seeing online — largely without seeking it out — and what young people want done about it.

items.title: Manitoba wait times for knee replacement surgery 2nd worst in country in 2025, data says
items.summary_auto: Manitobans are facing long waits for knee replacement surgeries, as new data shows only about three in 10 procedures completed last year were done within Canada's six-month benchmark.

items.title: Lawsuit filed in case of polluting, derelict N.S. tug that cost $4.1M to clean up
items.summary_auto: The alleged owner of a derelict former tugboat that was leaking oil in a rural harbour along Nova Scotia’s Eastern Shore is being sued over cleanup and dismantling costs that the Canadian Coast Guard claims top $4.1 million.

items.title: He managed Jesuit finances for decades. Now he has to repay the Catholic order $8.8M in stolen funds
Energy minister to name 5 priority power-line projects for federal backing today
items.summary_auto: The federal government is expected to announce on Friday that it will prioritize infrastructure upgrades that allow provinces to share excess electricity more cheaply and easily.

items.title: Northern Ontario leaders react to possible fast-tracking of nuclear waste repository
items.summary_auto: The federal government has announced Canada's proposed deep geological repository for nuclear waste in northwestern Ontario is possibly being listed as a project of national interest, which means the construction timeline for the $26-billion project could be accelerated under the Building Canada Act. The news is being welcomed by some leaders, who say the project is critical for the region’s economic revival.

items.title: Why Europe can't air condition its way out of extreme heat
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people are dead from Europe’s heatwave, in a continent where air conditioning is not the norm. But while ACs may save lives, they can also make the problem worse.

items.title: Supergirl is almost impressively boring. Almost.
items.summary_auto: Written by first-time screenwriter Ana Nogueira and directed by action-newbie Craig Gillespie, it is frankly a shock how directionless this follow-up to the DCU's 2025 eclectically energetic Superman is.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued for former B.C. correctional officer charged with breach of trust
Blue Jays' Ernie Clement into all-star game after receiving most American League fan votes
items.summary_auto: Toronto Blue Jays infielder Ernie Clement is heading to the MLB all-star game after finishing as the surprise top recipient of fan votes among American League players.

items.title: Heated Rivalry musical parody heads home to Canada, first stop: Toronto
items.summary_auto: Heated Rivalry: The Unauthorized Musical Parody is heading to Toronto for a limited run starting July 9, debuting just after Pride. Canadian director-producer Alan Kliffer says the show’s off-Broadway success started as a fandom-inspired idea to turn the hit Canadian TV series' romance into a musical parody. He hopes Toronto keeps the momentum going toward a full-scale Canadian production.
